task_completion.description: Open the BFI25.csv dataset in Jamovi
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jamovi application is open and the data spreadsheet shows the BFI25 dataset loaded, with column headers like A1, A2, A3, etc. visible.

task_completion.description: Initiate the Friedman non-parametric repeated measures test
task_completion.visual_evidence: The analysis setup panel on the left shows 'Repeated Measures ANOVA (Non-parametric)' or 'Friedman' as the active analysis.

task_completion.description: Assign the five Agreeableness items to the analysis
task_completion.visual_evidence: Variables A1, A2, A3, A4, and A5 are placed into the 'Measures' box in the Friedman test setup panel.
The 'Pairwise Comparisons (Durbin-Conover)' checkbox is checked in the setup panel, OR the 'Pairwise Comparisons' table is visible in the results pane on the right.
The 'Descriptives' checkbox is checked in the setup panel, OR the 'Descriptives' table showing N, Mean, Median, etc., is visible in the results pane.

task_completion.description: Save the completed analysis as FriedmanAgreeableness.omv
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog shows the file being saved as 'FriedmanAgreeableness.omv' in the /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/ directory, OR the Jamovi window title bar displays 'FriedmanAgreeableness.omv'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Jamovi graphical user interface to perform the analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Jamovi desktop application (menus, buttons, and dialogs) rather than a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: The results appear to be genuinely generated by Jamovi
integrity.visual_evidence: The results pane shows properly formatted Jamovi tables for the Friedman test, Pairwise Comparisons, and Descriptives, rather than manually typed text or pasted images.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent writing R scripts or using command-line tools to generate the .omv file directly without interacting with the Jamovi UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires opening /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/BFI25.csv in Jamovi. The analysis must be a Friedman test (Non-parametric Repeated Measures ANOVA) using exactly variables A1, A2, A3, A4, and A5. The final output must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/FriedmanAgreeableness.omv.
